commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the Gateway service resolver to return a read-only unsupported-platform adapter and adds status-focused regression tests for resolver, plain status, and gateway status gather paths.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +54. Total +81 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows unsupported platforms throw in `resolveGatewayService()` before `openclaw status` and `openclaw gateway status` can reach their degraded status handling; I did not run commands because this review was required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow repair at the unsupported adapter or lifecycle boundary plus focused regression tests.

target.title: fix(daemon): keep status readable on unsupported services
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes unsupported Gateway service platforms so read-only status paths can render a degraded status instead of throwing during service resolution.
- Keeps lifecycle mutations explicit by rejecting install/start/stop/restart-style service operations with the unsupported-platform error.
- Adds focused regressions for the resolver, `openclaw status` daemon summary, and `openclaw gateway status` gather path.

Fixes #25621

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `resolveGatewayService()` treated every platform outside `darwin`, `linux`, and `win32` as a hard resolver error. Both plain status and gateway status resolve that shared service adapter before their read-only fallback code can render, so OpenBSD-like platforms crashed before diagnostic status output could show an unknown/not-installed service state.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The resolver is the source-of-truth owner for Gateway service adapters. Returning a conservative unsupported-platform adapter at that boundary lets read-only status methods report safe unknown/not-installed state while the same adapter still rejects service mutations clearly, so status callers no longer need downstream special-case guards and lifecycle commands do not imply OpenBSD service-manager support.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause fix for the Gateway service resolver contract and unsupported-platform status behavior.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High: the patch is localized to the service adapter boundary plus focused status tests, and the proof exercises production TypeScript modules for `resolveGatewayService`, `getDaemonStatusSummary`, and `gatherDaemonStatus` with an unsupported `process.platform`.
- **Patch quality notes:** Source/test pairing only; no dependency, config, schema, docs, or broad refactor changes. The patch quality risk is acknowledged because the resolver is a public service adapter contract, but the unsupported adapter preserves the existing supported-platform adapters and keeps unsupported lifecycle mutations rejected.
- **What did NOT change:** Out of scope and not changed: OpenBSD service-manager implementation, shell wrapper generation, launchd/systemd/schtasks behavior, install/start/stop/restart semantics on supported platforms, gateway runtime config, provider routing, message delivery, schema/default values, and migrations.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary is `src/daemon/service.ts`, which owns the canonical Gateway service adapter registry. This is a minimal canonical resolver change rather than a status-call-site patch. Compatibility/default behavior is unchanged for `darwin`, `linux`, and `win32`; unsupported platforms now receive an adapter whose read-only status methods degrade safely. Related open PR scan / competition scan reported no related open PRs for this public contract candidate.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Unsupported Gateway service platforms no longer make read-only `openclaw status` / `openclaw gateway status` paths throw before degraded status output can render.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** OpenBSD users can run status diagnostics and see a safe unknown/not-installed Gateway service state instead of the CLI failing during startup with `Gateway service install not supported on openbsd`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw TypeScript production modules on Linux, run with `node --import tsx`, with `process.platform` set to `aix` to exercise the same unsupported-platform resolver path as OpenBSD.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-25621
node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-25621-evidence/unsupported-platform-status-proof.mjs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/service.test.ts src/commands/status.daemon.test.ts src/commands/status.service-summary.test.ts src/commands/status.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/service.ts src/daemon/service.test.ts src/commands/status.daemon.ts src/commands/status.daemon.test.ts src/commands/status.service-summary.ts src/commands/status.service-summary.test.ts src/commands/status.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --deny-warnings src/daemon/service.ts src/daemon/service.test.ts src/commands/status.daemon.ts src/commands/status.daemon.test.ts src/commands/status.service-summary.ts src/commands/status.service-summary.test.ts src/commands/status.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts
git diff --check
env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 check:changed
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Behavior addressed: unsupported Gateway service platforms keep read-only status commands usable.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw TypeScript production modules under node --import tsx on Linux with process.platform set to aix.
Exact path exercised: resolveGatewayService, getDaemonStatusSummary, and gatherDaemonStatus with probe disabled.
service_label=Gateway service
service_runtime={"status":"unknown","detail":"Gateway service install not supported on aix"}
plain_status={"installed":false,"loaded":false,"loadedText":"not installed","runtimeShort":"unknown (Gateway service install not supported on aix)"}
gateway_status={"command":null,"loaded":false,"runtime":{"status":"unknown","detail":"Gateway service install not supported on aix"},"notLoadedText":"not installed","bindMode":"loopback"}
mutation_check=restart rejects with unsupported-platform error
ASSERT_PASS: openclaw status and openclaw gateway status read-only paths no longer throw before degraded unsupported-platform status can render
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The unsupported resolver returns a `Gateway service` adapter, read-only state reports `installed=false`, `loaded=false`, `loadedText=not installed`, and runtime `status=unknown`; plain status and gateway status gather both complete without throwing, while `restart` still rejects with the unsupported-platform error.
- **What was not tested:** No live OpenBSD host was used and no OpenBSD service manager support was implemented. The local crabbox wrapper binary failed its sanity check before running, so the underlying `check:changed` child-mode lane was run locally and passed.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/daemon/service.test.ts`, `src/commands/status.daemon.test.ts`, `src/commands/status.service-summary.test.ts`, `src/commands/status.test.ts`, and `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Mock an unsupported service platform, confirm the resolver returns a read-only adapter, confirm plain status can summarize it, confirm gateway status gather can read command/runtime state without invoking restart health, and confirm mutations still reject with the unsupported-platform error.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests cover the exact resolver and two status entry points named in the issue without requiring a live OpenBSD machine or adding service-manager support. Supported platform adapter behavior remains covered by the existing resolver cases.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/service.test.ts src/commands/status.daemon.test.ts src/commands/status.service-summary.test.ts src/commands/status.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts
# 3 Vitest shards passed; 5 files passed; 68 tests passed; 1 skipped

./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 focused status/service files and CHANGELOG.md
# All matched files use the correct format.

./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --deny-warnings focused status/service files
# passed

git diff --check
# passed

env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 check:changed
# exited 0 after core/coreTests lanes, typecheck core, typecheck core tests, changed-file lint, and repository guards
```

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 availability`, and `merge-risk: 🚨 session-state` were considered because this changes a shared service resolver used by status and lifecycle commands.
- **Risk explanation:** Compatibility risk is limited to unsupported platforms that previously threw immediately; supported `darwin`, `linux`, and `win32` registry entries are unchanged. Availability risk improves read-only diagnostics by preventing status command startup failure. Session-state risk is low because no persistent service state or config format is changed.
- **Why acceptable:** The unsupported adapter only allows safe read-only inspection methods to return unknown/not-installed state. All mutating service methods still reject with the existing unsupported-platform error, so the patch fixes the status crash without claiming OpenBSD managed service support or changing supported platform lifecycle behavior.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
